title: SODIUM BUTYRATE
description: Sodium butyrate is the salt form of butyric acid, a short-chain fatty acid (SCFA) produced by the gut microbiota that acts as a potent histone deacetylase (HDAC) inhibitor. It modulates gene expression to improve mitochondrial function, regulate energy metabolism, and support intestinal barrier integrity.

research.title: Sodium butyrate epigenetically modulates high-fat diet-induced skeletal muscle mitochondrial adaptation, obesity and insulin resistance through nucleosome positioning
research.summary: This study demonstrates that sodium butyrate can prevent high-fat diet-induced obesity and insulin resistance by improving mitochondrial function and oxidative metabolism in skeletal muscle.

research.title: Sodium butyrate improves memory function in an Alzheimer's disease mouse model when administered at an advanced stage of disease progression
research.summary: Research found that sodium butyrate administration can restore memory function and improve cognitive performance even in late-stage neurodegenerative models by elevating histone acetylation.

research.title: Anti-obesity effect of butyrate links to modulation of gut microbiome and epigenetic regulation of muscular circadian clock
research.summary: Butyrate supplementation was found to reduce weight gain and enhance fatty acid oxidation by regulating the muscle's circadian clock genes and increasing histone acetylation.

research.title: Sodium butyrate improves memory and modulates the activity of histone deacetylases in aged rats after the administration of d-galactose
research.summary: This paper highlights butyrate's ability to reverse memory decline associated with aging and oxidative stress through the modulation of epigenetic markers.

sentiment.summary: Users generally view sodium butyrate as a powerful 'biological' tool for gut health and longevity, often reporting significant improvements in brain fog and digestive stability when combined with prebiotics.

effects.label: Obesity Prevention:
effects.description: Highly effective in animal models for preventing weight gain despite high-calorie intake.

effects.label: Cognitive Recovery:
effects.description: Shows significant promise in reversing symptoms of age-related memory loss and neurodegeneration.

effects.label: Insulin Sensitivity:
effects.description: Effectively promotes glucose homeostasis and reduces insulin resistance through skeletal muscle adaptations.

effects.label: Synergistic Potential:
effects.description: Works best when combined with dietary fiber (prebiotics) that fuel endogenous butyrate production.

dosage.label: Clinical Concentration:
dosage.description: Often studied in concentrations that achieve significant pan-histone acetylation in target tissues.

dosage.label: Dietary Supplementation:
dosage.description: Frequently taken in capsule form (often Tributyrin or Sodium Butyrate) to bypass early digestion.

dosage.label: Bioavailability Note:
dosage.description: Users often discuss 'scented' vs. 'unscented' versions, noting that the salt form has a distinct, strong odor.

dosage.label: Fiber Loading:
dosage.description: Many users aim for 20-30g of prebiotic fiber to naturally stimulate high internal levels of butyrate.

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Odor:
sideEffects.description: The most commonly reported 'side effect' is a strong, cheese-like odor in the supplement and occasionally in breath/sweat.

sideEffects.label: Digestive Discomfort:
sideEffects.description: High initial doses may cause mild cramping or changes in bowel habits as the microbiome shifts.

sideEffects.label: Electrolyte Balance:
sideEffects.description: As a sodium salt, users with hypertension may need to monitor total daily sodium intake.

availability.label: OTC Status:
availability.description: Widely available as an over-the-counter dietary supplement in many regions.

availability.label: Formulations:
availability.description: Available in standard sodium butyrate capsules, as well as more shelf-stable or time-released forms like Tributyrin.
